meta.purpose: C-series discharge routing correction, CT discharge timeline verification, QSO source status, and discharge workflow caveats

code_routing_corrections.reason: NORS Table 2 defines C01 as Admission, C02 as Appeal process, C03 as Discharge or eviction, and C04 as Room issues. Discharge portal, appeal, transfer/discharge handout, and CMS transfer/discharge resources should route to C01, C02, and C03, but not pure C04 room-change issues.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: C04 Room issues should not auto-trigger discharge or appeal resources unless the facts also show a discharge, eviction, or transfer notice issue.

resource_corrections.reason: The CT LTCOP Discharge Portal is a hub for involuntary discharge and transfer processes and should surface for C03 Discharge or eviction along with C01/C02 where admission or appeal issues are tied to discharge.
The involuntary discharge portal user manual is primarily a technical process resource for submitting involuntary transfer/discharge notices and aligns most directly with C03 Discharge or eviction.

The CT LTCOP transfers and discharges QSO-25-14-NH portal update deck focuses on transfer/discharge notices, F627/F628, and portal submission workflows, which primarily support C03 Discharge or eviction.
The NORC discharge handout addresses involuntary transfer/discharge rights and appeal options; C03 Discharge or eviction is the core discharge complaint code and should be included.
The Consumer Voice involuntary transfer/discharge fact sheet explains notice rules, permissible reasons, and appeal rights for discharge/eviction situations; C03 should be included.

resource_currency_rows.title: LTCOP Involuntary Discharge Portal - Website Help Manual for Facility Staff
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: Manual is dated December 2022 and predates CT Public Act 25-16 and CMS QSO-25-14-NH's 2025 transfer/discharge guidance; verify with CT LTCOP whether a newer help manual exists before relying on portal screenshots or workflow details.

resource_currency_rows.title: Transfers and Discharges - CMS QSO-25-14-NH and LTCOP Portal Updates
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: Slide deck reflects QSO-25-14-NH transfer/discharge guidance and CT portal expectations; confirm against later CMS QSO updates or CT statutory changes after 2025 before treating as fully current.

source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Primary source for defining C01 Admission, C02 Appeal process, C03 Discharge or eviction, and C04 Room issues when deciding which discharge-related resources should trigger for which NORS codes.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Provides NORS definitions and examples, not legal or regulatory text.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CGS 19a-535 - Nursing home transfer or discharge
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: State-law source for nursing facility transfer/discharge conditions, notice requirements, appeal process, and timelines, including 20-day stay deadline and 60-day hearing request deadline for most involuntary discharges.
source_verification_rows.limitations: CGA chapter page includes multiple sections; specific 19a-535 language must be located within the chapter and rechecked for amendments after 2024.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CGS 19a-535a - Residential care homes transfer/discharge appeals
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: State-law source for residential care home or managed residential community transfer/discharge appeals, including 10-day request deadline, 7-business-day hearing timing, and post-hearing decision timing.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Recent legislation may amend 19a-535a; CGA text should be checked for new subsections or changes affecting notice or reporting obligations.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CMS QSO-25-07-NH Revised LTC Surveyor Guidance
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Historical reference for psychotropic/unnecessary medication surveyor guidance changes; do not treat as current authority.
source_verification_rows.limitations: CMS lists QSO-25-07-NH as expired; later memos revise or incorporate its content.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CMS QSO-25-14-NH Revised LTC Surveyor Guidance
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Current CMS memo for surveyor guidance on admission, transfer, and discharge, including F627 Inappropriate Transfers and Discharges and F628 Transfer and Discharge Process.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Later CMS memos may supersede specific sections and should be checked periodically.

workflow_update_rows.guidance: For admission, transfer, and discharge complaints, use C01 Admission for admission-contract and admission-process issues, C02 Appeal process when the issue is absence, confusion, or failure to follow appeal procedures, and C03 Discharge or eviction for discharge/eviction planning, notice, procedure, implementation, or refusal to readmit. Discharge portal, CT appeal timelines, Consumer Voice/NORC transfer-discharge handouts, and CMS QSO-25-14-NH transfer/discharge guidance should display for C03 and may display for C01/C02 when the facts connect to discharge or eviction.
workflow_update_rows.do_not_say: Do not treat C04 Room issues as a discharge/eviction code or automatically show discharge appeal resources for pure room-location disputes unless the facts also show a discharge, eviction, or transfer notice issue.

workflow_update_rows.guidance: For nursing homes under CGS 19a-535, residents have 60 days from the notice date to request a hearing and 20 days from receipt to request a hearing to stay the discharge. For residential care homes or managed residential communities under CGS 19a-535a, residents have 10 days from receipt of notice to request a hearing; the hearing must occur within 7 business days, and the decision must be issued within 20 days after the hearing record closes.
workflow_update_rows.do_not_say: Do not tell residents they have 30 days to decide whether to appeal without mentioning the 20-day stay deadline for nursing homes and the 10-day appeal window for residential care homes.

tooltip_rows.label: Which C-code for Discharge?
tooltip_rows.short_help: C03 is the core discharge/eviction code; C01 and C02 may also apply.
tooltip_rows.details: Use C03 Discharge or eviction when the complaint is primarily about the discharge or eviction itself, including planning, notice, procedure, implementation, or refusal to readmit. Use C01 Admission for admission-contract or bed-hold disputes, and C02 Appeal process when the complaint is that the facility failed to provide, explain, or follow an appeal process.

tooltip_rows.label: CT Discharge Appeals Timing
tooltip_rows.short_help: Nursing homes: 20-day stay, 60-day appeal; MRCs: 10-day appeal.
tooltip_rows.details: For nursing facilities, CGS 19a-535 gives residents 60 days from the notice date to request a hearing, but they must request within 20 days of receiving the notice to stay the discharge while the hearing is pending. For residential care homes or managed residential communities, CGS 19a-535a requires appeals within 10 days of notice.

human_review_flags.issue: Currency of CT LTCOP portal user manual versus newer CT discharge laws and QSO guidance.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Ask CT LTCOP whether a more recent version of the Involuntary Discharge Portal Website Help Manual exists that reflects Public Act 25-16 and post-2022 changes. If a newer manual exists, update the resource row and mark the 2022 version as outdated or archive-only.

human_review_flags.issue: QSO-25-07-NH stable CMS URL remains unresolved; memo is marked expired.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Continue to treat QSO-25-07-NH as expired and not operative, using QSO-25-14-NH as the primary CMS memo for current transfer/discharge and F627/F628 surveyor guidance. Periodically re-check the CMS Policy and Memos index before promoting QSO-25-07-NH to high authority in the tool.
